meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Struggling to debug code? Learn how to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your development and build professional-grade apps.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs. Instead of manual debugging, use AI-first architecture. By designing your logic with Antigravity and executing repairs via Claude Code, you shift from 'fixing bugs' to 'architecting solutions.' This methodology reduces debugging time by 80%, allowing you to focus on building features that impress employers.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in a void of red text, a silent testament to a project that has stalled. For every student developer, the 'debugging wall' is a rite of passage—a moment where logic fails and frustration mounts. You feel like you’re fighting the machine rather than commanding it.

But what if you stopped treating bugs as failures and started treating them as design constraints? By integrating AI into your workflow, you move from a reactive 'fix-it' mindset to a proactive 'architect' mindset. This guide will teach you how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to turn complex errors into clear, actionable design improvements.

steps.title: Map Your Logic with Antigravity
steps.description: Before touching the code, visualize the data flow. Use Antigravity to define the intended state, ensuring the AI understands your architectural goals before it touches the implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current state of my application logic. Create a flow diagram that identifies where the data transformation breaks down and suggest a more robust state management pattern.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --context='current-directory' --task='Map the application flow and identify logic gaps'

steps.title: Isolate the Error Scope
steps.description: Don't ask AI to 'fix everything.' Narrow the scope to a single module or function to maintain control and ensure the AI provides precise, non-breaking code changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this specific function to be pure and side-effect free. Identify the boundary where the error originates and propose a modular fix.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --select='src/modules/data-processor.js' --task='Refactor for pure function compliance'

steps.title: Generate Test-Driven Repairs
steps.description: Instruct the AI to write the test case that fails first. This ensures the fix is validated against your specific edge cases, preventing regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that recreates the bug I am experiencing. Once the test fails, suggest the minimal code change to make it pass.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task='Generate a failing test case for the current bug and implement the fix'

steps.title: Review and Validate Implementation
steps.description: Always audit the AI-generated code. Use this step to learn why the previous code failed, turning the debugging process into a learning milestone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the root cause of this bug in plain English and compare the old implementation to the new one. Why is the new approach more resilient?
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task='Explain the architectural fix applied to the codebase'

steps.title: Finalize and Integrate
steps.description: Merge the fix back into your main branch. By now, you aren't just fixing a bug; you've improved your system architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire module for consistency after the fix. Ensure all comments are updated to reflect the new logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task='Verify full module consistency and update documentation'

pro_tips: Always define your 'Success State' explicitly in Antigravity before asking for a code fix.
Treat Claude Code as a junior developer; provide context, constraints, and your desired style guide for every command.
Use 'dry-run' flags whenever possible to see what the AI plans to change before applying it to your filesystem.
If the AI suggests a complex fix, ask it to 'simplify the architecture' to avoid over-engineering your first project.
Keep a 'Debug Log' file. Every time you solve a bug with AI, document the error and the fix to build your personal knowledge base.
content.next_steps: Once your code is stable, deploy your project to a platform like Vercel or Railway to see your work live. Then, document the process in a blog post to showcase your problem-solving skills to potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a coder; you are an AI-augmented builder capable of shipping production-ready software. Don't let bugs hold you back from your potential. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
